title: narcissus pseudonarcissus subsp. abscissus
intro: Narcissus pseudonarcissus subsp. abscissus, also known as the wild daffodil or Lent lily, is a plant species within the Narcissus genus, characterized by its small, bell-shaped flowers and strap-like leaves. The medicinal properties of Narcissus pseudonarcissus subsp. abscissus include its ability to stimulate digestion and relieve symptoms of bloating and gas. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its antiseptic and antispasmodic properties. Herbal preparations of Narcissus pseudonarcissus subsp. abscissus include infusions and tinctures, which can be made by steeping the flowers and leaves in hot water or a solvent like ethanol, respectively.
